title: leptopteris wilkesiana
intro: Leptopteris wilkesiana, also known as the New Zealand fern, is a type of vascular fern plant native to New Zealand and Lord Howe Island. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Leptopteris wilkesiana has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Leptopteris wilkesiana is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its dried leaves can be used as a herbal remedy in various forms.
